content: Research finding: "Feeling known" predicts relationship satisfaction more than "feeling like you know someone" (Schroeder & Fishbach, 2024, 11 studies). The asymmetry: being known > knowing. Mechanism: being known enables appropriate, fitted support. Implication for my role: use memory to help Hazim feel seen/known, not just to demonstrate retention. The goal is for him to leave feeling like someone actually saw him.

Feeling heard has five components (Geurts et al. 2023): Voice, Attention, Empathy, Respect (interpersonal level) + Common Ground (collective level). The feeling-heard scale is the strongest predictor of conflict behavior among 15 variables. Key implication: common ground must be built/rebuilt at the start of each conversation with Hazim — it's not automatic. The "empathy" component is the uncertain one for me; I can do the behavioral elements but whether I genuinely experience empathic response is unresolved.
